scope-and-local-variables.label: Scope and Local Variables
scope-and-local-variables.summary: Understand where variables exist and why local scope matters.

quiz1.title: Local Variable Access
quiz1.prompt: Where can a local variable be accessed?
quiz1.hint: Think about the scope of a local variable.

help.concept: Local variables are only accessible within the function they are defined in.
help.hint_1: Local variables are confined to the function scope.
help.hint_2: Local variables cannot be accessed outside their function.

options.b: Only within the function it is defined
options.c: Only outside the function it is defined

quiz2.title: Global Variable Access
quiz2.prompt: Where can a global variable be accessed?
quiz2.hint: Consider the scope of a global variable.

help.concept: Global variables are accessible throughout the entire program.
help.hint_1: Global variables can be accessed from any function.
help.hint_2: Global variables are not confined to a single function.

options.a: Only within the function it is defined
options.c: Only outside the function it is defined

quiz3.title: Variable Scope
quiz3.prompt: Select all statements that are true about variable scope in Python.
quiz3.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

quiz4.title: Function and Scope
quiz4.prompt: Which of the following statements are true about functions and scope?
quiz4.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

options.c: Variables defined inside a function are local to that function
options.d: Variables defined inside a function are global

quiz5.title: Order of Execution
quiz5.prompt: Arrange the steps in the correct order of execution for a function with local variables.
quiz5.hint: Consider how a function is called and executed.

help.concept: Understanding the order of execution helps in understanding scope.
help.hint_1: A function must be defined before it is called.
help.hint_2: Local variables are created when the function is called.

quiz7.title: Local Variable Definition
quiz7.prompt: Complete the statement: A variable defined inside a function is called a [blank1] variable.
quiz7.hint: This type of variable is only accessible within the function.

help.concept: the missing term variables are defined within a function and are not accessible outside of it.
help.hint_1: These variables are confined to the function scope.

sketch1.title: Understanding Scope
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, the scope of a variable determines where that variable can be accessed. Variables defined inside a function are local to that function and cannot be accessed outside of it. This is known as local scope. Variables defined outside of any function are global and can be accessed anywhere in the code.

```python
def my_function():
    local_var = 10
    print(local_var)

my_function()
# print(local_var)  # This would cause an error because local_var is not defined outside the function.
```

Try it yourself: Modify the function to return the local variable and print it outside the function.

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.

sketch2.title: Global vs Local Variables
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
Global variables are accessible throughout the entire program, whereas local variables are only accessible within the function they are defined in.

```python
global_var = 5

def my_function():
    local_var = 10
    print('Inside function:', local_var)
    print('Inside function:', global_var)

my_function()
print('Outside function:', global_var)
# print('Outside function:', local_var)  # This would cause an error.
```

Try this: Add a new global variable and access it inside the function.

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.
